items.title: The Better Trap
items.angle: Stronger models make local work feel magical, but enterprise software delivery still fails when process, testing, and ownership stay weak.
items.notes: Seed review pending before any research or drafting.

items.title: How Coding Agents Turned My ADHD Into a Superpower
items.angle: ADHD felt like a curse when every new idea outpaced my ability to focus, finish, and ship. Coding agents flipped that weakness into leverage: I can now steer multiple projects at once while Copilot CLI handles seed ideas, research, planning, execution, and testing across each phase.
items.notes: Seed review pending before any research or drafting.

items.title: The V-Model Is Back
items.angle: As coding gets cheaper, the enterprise bottleneck swings back to intent, validation, and cross-functional test discipline.

items.title: The Trust Pendulum
items.angle: Customers chase AI speed until a security or quality scare snaps priorities back toward trust, governance, and control.

items.title: Commando Teams Don’t Scale
items.angle: Small expert teams can prove agentic engineering works, but platform and governance are what make it repeatable across an enterprise.
items.notes: Seed review pending before any research or drafting.

items.title: How Non-Coders Can Build Coding Muscle With Copilot CLI
items.angle: Non-coders do not need to become full-time developers to build real coding muscle. Spending just an hour a day with Copilot CLI to brainstorm, research, prototype, and create things that are not necessarily code can build confidence, pattern recognition, and an AI-first mindset that compounds over time.
items.notes: Seed review pending before any research or drafting.

items.title: Atomic Habits for Agentic Engineering
items.angle: Atomic Habits shaped how I think about agentic engineering: the biggest leaps rarely come from one breakthrough, but from improving systems and skills 1% at a time. The real lesson is to build systems, not just chase goals. Start building an agent skill today, and the compounding effect of small daily improvements will eventually feel unbelievable.
